evidence_quotes.quote: Opt-in auto-updates Products that came with software or are software must NOT have automated updates enabled unless the user explicitly opts-in.
customer MUST be able to install applications from where they seem fit not from a preset store.

the initial Agreement that was effective at the moment of purchase should apply unless the customer explicitly agrees on a newer agreement on an opt-in basis that must be clearly and transparently shown.
use the word BUY or PURCHASE for products that the customer would buy and own forever

paragraphs.text: It would be awesome if the D.F.A. could assure the following: 1. Transparent and clear way of expressing what are you buying/licensing Companies would be need to use the word BUY or PURCHASE for products that the customer would buy and own forever, and to use the word RENT, SUBSCRIBE or LICENCE for licences to products wether perpetual or time-limited. 2. No agreement changes after purchase When buying or licensing a product only the initial Agreement that was effective at the moment of purchase should apply unless the customer explicitly agrees on a newer agreement on an opt-in basis that must be clearly and transparently shown. 3. No hardware shutdowns When consumer would buy (NOT license) any product the company must NOT be able to disable or brick the device in any way. Companies should be able to disable services offered with the purchase but must NOT be able to disable the device. (E.g. Nintendo has this in their EULA - such anti-consumer thing should not exist in modern society because it is legal theft) 4. Opt-in auto-updates Products that came with software or are software must NOT have automated updates enabled unless the user explicitly opts-in. Changes to firmware and firmare updates disabling certain features so the customer needs to pay for them have happend already. 5. Customer must be able to control their device If a customer for example buys a phone/computer or other device the customer MUST be able to switch to different operating system/revert to older firmware or install a different one. Same applies to intsalling applications, customer MUST be able to install applications from where they seem fit not from a preset store. (E.g. What Google is attempting and pushing into Android blocking installing applications from everywhere than Google Play) For more information, the link here presents records of companies behaving in anti-consumer manners. Even if most of this is happening in USA, it might encourage companies to try some of these things here as well and they are really creative to fu## over with customers. https://consumerrights.wiki/w/Main_Page
